name: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad
summary: The largest Upanishad, containing dialogues of Yajnavalkya with his wife Maitreyi and King Janaka. It teaches neti neti (not this, not this) as the method of negating all that is not Brahman, and declares aham brahmasmi — I am Brahman.

verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अहम् — प्रत्यगात्मा — ब्रह्म — परमात्मा — अस्मि — अभिन्नः। इदम् महावाक्यम् — शुक्लयजुर्वेदस्य।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: 'I' — the individual Self — 'am' — identical with — 'Brahman' — the supreme Self. This is the mahavakya of the Shukla Yajurveda, declaring the non-difference of the individual and the universal Self.

verses.sanskrit: स एष नेति नेत्यात्मा | अगृह्यो न हि गृह्यते | अशीर्यो न हि शीर्यते | असङ्गो न हि सज्यते | असितो न व्यथते | न रिष्यति ||३.९.२६||
verses.transliteration: sa eṣa neti nety ātmā | agṛhyo na hi gṛhyate | aśīryo na hi śīryate | asaṅgo na hi sajyate | asito na vyathate | na riṣyati ||3.9.26||
verses.english_translation: This Self is: not this, not this. It is ungraspable, for it cannot be grasped. It is indestructible, for it does not decay. It is unattached, for it does not attach. It is unfettered, it does not suffer, it is not injured.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: नेति नेति — सर्वविशेषनिषेधेन ब्रह्म निरूप्यते। यत् किञ्चित् उपाधिः — तत् ब्रह्म न।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: By the method of 'not this, not this,' Brahman is described by negating all limiting adjuncts. Whatever can be pointed to as 'this' — that is not Brahman. Brahman is the pure witness beyond all objectification.

verses.sanskrit: न वा अरे पत्युः कामाय पतिः प्रियो भवति | आत्मनस्तु कामाय पतिः प्रियो भवति ||२.४.५||
verses.transliteration: na vā are patyuḥ kāmāya patiḥ priyo bhavati | ātmanas tu kāmāya patiḥ priyo bhavati ||2.4.5||

word_meanings.meaning: for the sake of the Self

verses.english_translation: Verily, it is not for the husband's sake that the husband is dear, but for the sake of the Self that the husband is dear.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पत्युः कामाय — पत्युः स्वार्थाय — पतिः प्रियः न भवति। आत्मनः कामाय — स्वस्य आत्मनः प्रीत्यर्थम् — पतिः प्रियः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Yajnavalkya teaches Maitreyi that all love is ultimately love of the Self. The husband, wife, children, wealth — all are dear not for their own sake but because the Self shines through them. The Self alone is the ultimate object of all love.
